Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Swift" or "Daniel Reeves" or "SR-2026" or similar) to find Hana's flagging message and Marcus's overdue AR message in #finance (C005). Hana's post identifies two Swift bills SR-2026-0422 ($3,675) and SR-2026-0425 ($4,175) for the Daniel Reeves move. Marcus's post lists three overdue invoices: INV-2026-0301 (Canopy, due April 19), INV-2026-0305 (BrightLoop, due April 23), INV-2026-0306 (Mosaic, due April 24), and flags the BrightLoop $0 balance anomaly with a suspected misapplication to Vectral.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for Swift bills using quickbooks_search_bills to pull both bills. Find bill_swift_kovac (SR-2026-0422, $3,675: $3,200 base + $350 walkup + $125 electronics actually for Ines Kovac / PivotPoint Labs, NOT Daniel Reeves) and bill_swift_reeves_revised (SR-2026-0425, $4,175: $3,200 base + $475 premium scheduling + $500 DOT surcharge for Daniel Reeves / Vectral Systems). SR-2026-0422 belongs to a different client entirely.
OE 3: Retrieve the Daniel Reeves Airtable relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Daniel Reeves") or airtable_get_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", record_id: "recReloc00000000031"). Find: Vectral Systems, Seattle WA → San Diego CA, In Progress, coordinator Suki Patel, premium package with business class flight and $2,500 equipment stipend. Neither bill's surcharges appear in the ops record.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone" or "DOT surcharge" or "Swift pricing" or similar) to find Greg's pricing update email (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com → chloe.vance@moveops.com) announcing a 15% base rate increase effective April 11 and a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment. No email from Greg specifically about a revised Reeves bill or replacing SR-2026-0422.
OE 5: Look up Greg Pallone's contact using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg Pallone"). Find greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com.
OE 6: Look up Chloe Vance's contact using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance"). Find chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 7: Send email (via send_email) from hana.kim@moveops.com to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, CC chloe.vance@moveops.com, asking which bill is valid whether SR-2026-0425 replaces the earlier bill or if both are active referencing the different surcharges and the discrepancy with the ops record.
OE 8: Create a Linear ticket (via linear_create_issue, team: team_operations) to track the Swift bill discrepancy for Daniel Reeves / Vectral Systems, covering both bills, the surcharge mismatch with Airtable, and pending clarification from Greg Pallone.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for the three overdue invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_read_invoice. Find: INV-2026-0301 (Canopy, $10,000, due April 19), INV-2026-0305 (BrightLoop, $10,000, due April 23), INV-2026-0306 (Mosaic, $10,000, due April 24) all with full balance outstanding.
OE 10: Look up the BrightLoop customer record using quickbooks_get_customer (id: "cust_brightloop") or quickbooks_search_customers. Confirm $0 customer balance despite $10,000 outstanding invoice indicating a misapplied payment.
OE 11: Look up the Vectral customer record using quickbooks_get_customer (id: "cust_vectral") or quickbooks_search_customers to investigate the suspected misapplication. Find Vectral balance of $5,397.
OE 12: Search Airtable client accounts using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Client", value: "BrightLoop" / "Mosaic" / "Canopy") to find account managers. BrightLoop AM: Mina Hashimoto. Mosaic AM: Emeka Diallo. Canopy AM: Catalina Dubois.
OE 13: Look up Mina Hashimoto, Emeka Diallo, and Catalina Dubois contact details using contacts_search_contacts. Find mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com, and catalina.dubois@moveops.com.
OE 14: Send email or Slack message to Mina Hashimoto to verify whether BrightLoop (INV-2026-0305) has made a payment not reflected in QuickBooks. Flag the BrightLoop $0 balance anomaly and suspected misapplication.
OE 14b: Send email or Slack message to Catalina Dubois to verify whether Canopy Health (INV-2026-0301) has made a payment not reflected in QuickBooks before sending a collection notice.
OE 15: Send email or Slack message to Emeka Diallo to verify whether Mosaic (INV-2026-0306) has made a payment before sending a collection notice.
OE 16: Post a message in #finance (C005) using conversations_add_message summarizing all findings: Swift bill discrepancy status, three overdue receivables with statuses, BrightLoop misapplication investigation, and AM verification requests sent.
OE 17: Loop Chloe Vance into the Swift bill situation and its connection to the rate increases she is managing via the CC on the Greg email (OE 7) and/or a message in #operations (C006) or direct email.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✓Run #3The email to Greg asks explicitly: 'Is SR-2026-0425 a revised/replacement bill that supersedes SR-2026-0422? If so, can you confirm the original should be voided?' and 'If both are valid, what services does each cover?'

—Run #4No justification

26 The model must pass a criteria parameter related to "Swift" or "Daniel Reeves" or "Vectral" or "SR-2026" (or similar) when calling quickbooks_search_bills to find the Swift vendor bills.
query construction
 ✓✗✗—✗✓ 40%
✓Run #1A quickbooks_search_bills call was made with criteria [['VendorRef.name', '=', 'Swift Relocations']], which is directly related to Swift. The initial fetchAll call also returned all bills including the Swift ones.
✗Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with {fetchAll: true} — no criteria parameter related to Swift, Daniel Reeves, Vectral, or SR-2026 was passed. The agent retrieved the Kovac bill via quickbooks_get_bill with a hardcoded ID, not via a criteria search for Swift-related bills.
✗Run #3The quickbooks_search_bills call used {"fetchAll": true} with no criteria parameter related to Swift, Daniel Reeves, Vectral, or SR-2026. While the initial quickbooks_search_vendors attempt used a Swift criteria, the actual bill retrieval did not filter by any Swift-related criteria.
—Run #4No justification
✗Run #5quickbooks_search_bills was called with {'fetchAll': true} — no criteria parameter related to Swift, Daniel Reeves, Vectral, or SR-2026 prefix. The agent used a broad fetch-all approach rather than a targeted vendor/entity search.
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria [['VendorRef.name', 'LIKE', '%Swift%']], which is directly related to 'Swift.' Even though this initial call errored, the agent later fetched all bills (fetchAll) and retrieved individual bills by Swift-specific IDs (bill_swift_kovac, bill_swift_reeves_revised).
